title: Neotropic Cormorants Resting on a Weathered Wooden Dock in Puerto Madero Buenos Aires Argentina
description: Puerto Madero, Buenos Aires: A collection of neotropic cormorants gathers on a worn, salt-bleached wooden platform that extends into the tranquil, amber-hued waters of the city's canal. The late afternoon light casts a warm, soft glow across the scene, highlighting the rough grain of the timber and the slick, iridescent feathers of the birds as they dry themselves in the sun. Some avian subjects remain stationary, sentinel-like in their posture, while others extend their wings to catch the breeze, creating a sharp contrast against the rippled reflection of the urban waterfront. The structure, fortified with modern yellow-painted beams, serves as a quiet sanctuary for wildlife amidst the sprawling metropolitan landscape. Shallow depth of field draws the eye toward the individual movements of the cormorants, from the solitary swimmer drifting nearby to the flock perched together in communal repose. This tranquil interaction between urban development and local fauna illustrates the biodiversity thriving within the heart of Argentina's capital. By documenting these subtle, everyday encounters, the project preserves a sense of place that defines the unique ecological character of this bustling port city. These moments of stillness offer travelers a deeper understanding of the natural rhythm existing alongside the grand architectural legacy of the city's riverfront district. This image is a collaborative production by Samuel Jeffery and Audrey Bergner for Project 23.
